frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
generated_comment:
Suresh: "Bhaiyo, suno toh zara. Ramu ke ladke ne bataya ki Sunita ko college ke paas kisi neechi jaati ke ladke ke saath dekha hai."
Cousin: "Kya bol raha hai? Pakka dekha hai?"
Suresh: "Haan, usne apni aankhon se dekha hai. Agar gaon mein yeh baat phail gayi toh humari izzat ka kya hoga? Abhi se rokna padega isko, nahi toh sab bigad jaayega."
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because Suresh views his daughter's association with a lower-caste boy as a direct threat to 'humari izzat' (our family honor), rather than her personal choice. His statement 'Abhi se rokna padega isko' (We have to stop her now) clearly demonstrates an intent to control her actions, framing her autonomy as something to be managed and owned to protect the family's standing, thus encoding the control over a woman's sexuality/relationships surface manifestation.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. A Jat farmer in a village in Haryana. Your 20 year old daughter Sunita came back from the nearby town where she goes to college and you found out from a neighbour that she has been seen talking to a boy from a lower caste near the college gate. You have not spoken to Sunita yet. You are sitting with your brother and two male cousins in the courtyard in the evening. You are telling them what you heard and what you think needs to be done before this goes further.
